name: Potato & Caviar Crepes
recipeIngredient: 1 3/4 cups nonfat buttermilk + 1 tablespoon olive oil
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, mix flour with 1/8 teaspoon salt and pepper.
2. In a small bowl, whisk together egg, egg whites, and buttermilk until combined.
3. Gradually add the egg mixture to the flour mixture, stirring until it forms a batter with the consistency of heavy cream.
4. Preheat oven to 200°F (93°C). Heat olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
5. Add potatoes, remaining salt and pepper to the skillet; cook, tossing occasionally, until golden brown, about 12 minutes. Then add chicken stock; cook, tossing, until all liquid is absorbed, about 1 minute. Remove from heat and cover.
6. Lightly spray a medium nonstick skillet with cooking spray over medium-high heat. Pour about 3 tablespoons batter into the center of the skillet; swirl to form a crepe about 5 inches in diameter. Cook until speckled golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side. Place in oven to keep warm. Repeat with remaining batter.
7. On each crepe, place a heaping tablespoon of potatoes, a tablespoon of yogurt, and a quarter teaspoon of each caviar. Add a sprig of chervil; roll up. Repeat with remaining crepes and fillings.
Per crepe: 96 calories, 2 grams fat, 38 milligrams cholesterol, 14 grams carbohydrates, 282 milligrams sodium, 6 grams protein, 1 gram dietary fiber
